title: DNA extraction protocol for the thraustochytrid Aurantiochytrium limacinum
author: Ben Leyland

steps.title: Colonies should be grown on an...
steps.instruction: Colonies should be grown on an agar-media plate for 4 days, and no older than 2 months.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of DNA Extraction...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of DNA Extraction Buffer to a PCR tube.

steps.title: Scrape some colony using a plastic pipette tip
steps.instruction: Scrape some colony using a plastic pipette tip.

steps.title: Resuspend the colony in the 20 µL DNA Extraction Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend the colony in the 20 µL DNA Extraction Buffer.

steps.title: Immediately remove PCR tube from thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Immediately remove PCR tube from thermocycler, and allow to cool to room temperature on the benchtop. [Room temperature cooling is important for several reasons. It allows for re-annealing of DNA to the more stable dsDNA form, as well as the precipitation of cellular debris.]

steps.title: Once cooled to room temperature, add...
steps.instruction: Once cooled to room temperature, add 80 µL of sterile nuclease-free deionized water, and mix by gently pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Sample is now ready for use
steps.instruction: Sample is now ready for use. 1-2 µL should be sufficient for a 50 µL PCR reaction. If not using, freeze at -20°C. For temporary storage, keep on ice, or in ice water, or at 4°C.
